title: Lumbar Vertebrae and Intervertebral Disc – Sagittal View
description: Sagittal section from Gray's Anatomy of lumbar vertebrae and intervertebral discs showing the nucleus pulposus centrally within the annulus fibrosus, the spinal ligaments, and the large vertebral bodies designed for weight bearing. The posterior facet joints guide and limit lumbar motion.
clinical_notes: L4–L5 and L5–S1 are most common herniation levels. OT role: body mechanics education, back school programs, ergonomic assessment, adaptive equipment for ADL with lumbar restrictions, seated posture and seating assessment.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 7 – The Axial Skeleton (Unit 2: Support and Movement)
